title: UBS reiterates Packaging Corp. of America stock buy on guidance miss risk
summary: UBS has reiterated a Buy rating and a $248.00 price target for Packaging Corp. of America (NYSE:PKG), despite the company indicating a potential miss on its second-quarter earnings guidance. The company cited higher freight and recycled fiber costs, as well as lower outside sales and exports, as reasons for the expected shortfall. However, UBS maintains its positive outlook alongside other analysts who have also reiterated Buy ratings and increased price targets due to the company's pricing power, integration synergies, and strong demand outlook.

title: Oppenheimer raises Tesla stock price target on storage demand
summary: Oppenheimer has increased its price target for Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) due to anticipated higher demand for stationary storage and increased vehicle sales. The firm attributes this growth to Tesla's product evolution, cost reductions, and the supportive effect of elevated oil prices on global EV demand. Additionally, Oppenheimer noted that speculation regarding a potential merger with SPCX may provide some support for Tesla shares, recognizing CEO Elon Musk's strategy for diversified capital access with two public currencies.

title: Is the 2026 Toyota bZ Woodland a Good Crossover EV? 5 Pros, 2 Cons
summary: The 2026 Toyota bZ Woodland is an electric crossover aimed at active-lifestyle buyers, offering increased space, all-wheel drive, and 375 horsepower. It shares many characteristics with the Subaru Trailseeker but adds 6 inches in length for more cargo and passenger room. While it boasts quick acceleration and decent range, its interior design and higher price point compared to the Trailseeker are noted as potential downsides.

title: General Motors pivots battery strategy, puts LMR ahead of widely used LFP - report (GM:NYSE)
summary: General Motors is reportedly shifting its electric vehicle battery strategy away from lithium iron phosphate (LFP) towards lithium manganese-rich (LMR) technology. This move suggests a significant change in GM's approach to EV chemistry. The report, citing Reuters, indicates a pivot in the company's long-term battery plans.

title: Musk to discuss Terafab chip plant plans at ASML event (ASML:NASDAQ)
summary: Elon Musk is scheduled to virtually attend ASML's annual technology conference on Thursday. He is expected to discuss Terafab, his semiconductor manufacturing venture. This initiative aims to produce chips for Tesla and SpaceX.

title: PepsiCo, Inc. $PEP Shares Acquired by Capital Wealth Planning LLC
summary: Capital Wealth Planning LLC significantly increased its stake in PepsiCo, Inc. (NASDAQ:PEP) during the fourth quarter, buying an additional 7,739 shares to hold a total of 20,703 shares valued at approximately $2.97 million. Institutional investors and hedge funds own 73.07% of PepsiCo's stock. The company recently reported strong quarterly results, increased its dividend to $1.48 per share, and is rated a consensus "Hold" by analysts with an average price target of $169.58.

title: Cathie Wood buys $4.3 million of tumbling tech stock
summary: Cathie Wood's Ark Investment Management recently purchased $4.3 million worth of DoorDash (DASH) shares after the stock experienced a significant decline. This purchase aligns with Wood's strategy of buying into high-tech companies following selloffs, despite her flagship Ark Innovation ETF (ARKK) currently underperforming the S&P 500 this year and showing negative long-term annualized returns. Wood also anticipates a Federal Reserve rate cut, believing new chair Kevin Warsh will reduce rates due to increased productivity from technology and moderating inflation.

title: Cathie Wood Asks Elon Musk How Tesla Handles 'Parking And Traffic Violations' After The Robotaxi She Rode Gets $75 Ticket
summary: Cathie Wood, CEO of ARK Invest, posed a question to Elon Musk regarding how Tesla plans to manage parking and traffic violations for its Robotaxi fleet after a driverless Tesla she rode in Austin received a $75 parking ticket. Wood indicated that this new operating expense would be added to ARK's valuation model for Tesla, highlighting a real-world cost that autonomous vehicle operators must address. Despite the ticket, she praised the smooth performance of Tesla's autonomous service compared to other robotaxi experiences.

title: Why SpaceX (SPCX) Could Trade Like Tesla On Steroids
summary: The article discusses how SpaceX (SPCX), set to trade on Nasdaq, could exhibit similar high volatility and narrative-driven trading patterns as Tesla (TSLA). It highlights factors like scarcity, a devoted retail base, a visionary founder (Elon Musk), and ambitious future plans (Starship, Mars, Starlink) contributing to an expected $1.75 trillion valuation, making it susceptible to significant price swings. The author also cautions investors about leveraged ETFs like SPCU, which aim to double SpaceX's daily moves, urging careful consideration of their inherent risks.

title: Sylebra Capital LLC, an Impinj director, sells $8.3 million in stock
summary: Sylebra Capital LLC, a director of Impinj Inc. (NASDAQ:PI), has sold approximately $8.3 million worth of common stock over two days, disposing of 64,633 shares at prices ranging from $125.9651 to $130.0685. These transactions occurred amidst a 9.2% weekly decline and a 28% year-to-date drop in Impinj's stock, which InvestingPro assesses as currently overvalued. Despite the stock sale, Impinj recently reported strong first-quarter 2026 earnings, exceeding analyst expectations for both EPS and revenue, and UBS raised its price target for the company to $175, citing improved inventory management and strengthening demand drivers.

title: Sociedad Química y Minera de Chile (SQM) Is Down 5.5% After Strong Q1 Beat And Higher Lithium Guidance
summary: Sociedad Química y Minera de Chile (SQM) reported strong Q1 2026 results with increased sales and net income, alongside higher lithium sales volumes and raised full-year guidance. Despite this positive performance, the stock is down 5.5%, highlighting investor concerns about lithium price volatility and potential oversupply. The company's investment narrative projects significant revenue and earnings growth by 2028, leading to a fair value estimate in line with its current price.

title: Tesla’s Robotaxi Falls Short With Long Waits and Stalled Rides
summary: Despite Elon Musk's ambitious promises to have Tesla robotaxis cover half the US population by late 2025, the company's fleet remains limited to just 59 vehicles operating in three Texas cities. Early user experiences are plagued by long waits and stalled rides, indicating the service is far from the broad rollout Musk initially envisioned.

title: Arteris president and CEO Charles Janac sells $2.44 million in stock
summary: Charles Janac, President and CEO of Arteris Inc., sold 70,000 shares of common stock totaling approximately $2.44 million on June 8, 2026. These sales, executed via a pre-arranged 10b5-1 trading plan, occurred after a significant stock appreciation but also a recent decline, with the stock appearing overvalued according to InvestingPro. Arteris recently exceeded Q1 2026 financial expectations and received a raised price target from TD Cowen due to strong AI growth and automotive performance.

title: Tesla robotaxis stall as Musk’s self-driving hype hits real-world traffic
summary: Tesla's highly anticipated robotaxi service, launched by Elon Musk in July 2025 with big promises of rapid expansion, has significantly underperformed expectations. Almost a year later, the fleet numbers only 59 vehicles operating in three Texas cities, a far cry from the projected "half the population of the U.S." and "500 or more" in Austin alone. The article highlights user frustrations with long wait times, limited availability, and inconsistent drop-off locations, suggesting the technology isn't yet ready for widespread deployment despite Musk's ambitious claims.

title: East West Bancorp stock hits all-time high at 130.19 USD
summary: East West Bancorp Inc. (EWBC) stock reached an all-time high of $130.19, reflecting a 38.51% increase over the past year. Despite trading just below its 52-week high, InvestingPro data suggests the company remains undervalued. The strong performance follows positive first-quarter 2026 financial results, which surpassed earnings and revenue expectations, leading to several analysts raising their price targets and maintaining "Buy" or "Overweight" ratings.

title: Musk’s jet travels highlight Texas, global business moves. Here's where he went.
summary: Elon Musk's early 2026 activities involved significant travel related to his companies, Tesla and SpaceX, and other ventures. His private jet logged

title: BYD outsold Ford, and now it thinks it can catch Toyota as the world’s No. 1 automaker
summary: BYD, already the world’s largest EV maker, is confident it will become the leading global automaker within five years, aiming to surpass Toyota. Having sold over 4.6 million new energy vehicles last year and exceeding Ford in sales, BYD is expanding its overseas presence and deploying new battery and charging technologies. While other traditional automakers delay EV projects, BYD builds most components in-house and plans a significant rollout of its Flash Charging stations across Europe.

title: Tesla Has 'Effectively' Achieved Level 4 Autonomy, Says Piper Sandler Analyst – How A Trip To Minneapolis Strengthened His Conviction
summary: Piper Sandler analyst Alexander Potter believes Tesla has "effectively" achieved Level 4 autonomy, citing the company's FSD subscriptions, robotaxi expansion, and plans for the Cybercab without a steering wheel. This conviction was reinforced by a trip where his Tesla drove him from Montana to Minneapolis. Tesla is also expanding FSD Supervised in Europe, while JPMorgan highlights Tesla's "unmatched" advantage in manufacturing and AI.

title: Centene and UFP Industries have been highlighted as Zacks Bull and Bear of the Day
summary: Zacks Equity Research has named Centene Corporation (CNC) as its "Bull of the Day" due to its impressive turnaround, strong financial performance, and positive earnings outlook in the healthcare sector. Conversely, UFP Industries (UFPI) has been designated as the "Bear of the Day" due to significant vulnerabilities, broad-based demand weakness, and ongoing margin compression stemming from its ties to the struggling housing and construction markets. The article also includes a discussion on SpaceX's anticipated IPO, comparing it to Tesla's market entry while highlighting the potential risks associated with its high valuation.

title: From $4 To $500? Eric Jackson's Bold Thesis Pitches Opendoor As Real Estate's Tesla Moment
summary: Eric Jackson, founder of EMJ Capital, suggests that Opendoor (NASDAQ: OPEN) could achieve a "$500 stock" valuation within five to seven years, representing an 11,420.73% upside from its current trading price. This bold prediction is based on his belief that Opendoor is uniquely positioned to revolutionize the housing market through the tokenization of real estate, separating land ownership from physical structures to lower homeownership costs and attract institutional capital. Jackson posits that Opendoor, unlike its competitors, possesses critical advantages such as asset-class control, pricing-data depth, vertical integration, and leadership with "crypto-native architecture experience," making it a generational investment opportunity similar to early Tesla and Shopify.

title: Centene and UFP Industries have been highlighted as Zacks Bull and Bear of the Day
summary: Zacks Equity Research has named Centene Corporation (CNC) as its Bull of the Day due to its impressive turnaround, recovering margins, and strong earnings estimates, positioning it for continued outperformance in the healthcare sector. Conversely, UFP Industries (UFPI) has been designated the Bear of the Day, grappling with broad demand weakness, falling volumes, and margin compression across all segments, driven by challenges in the housing and construction markets. The article also provides a speculative discussion on SpaceX's upcoming IPO, comparing its potential to Tesla's past success.

title: Tesla struggles with long robotaxi wait times in Austin with less than 60 vehicles in operation (TSLA:NASDAQ)
summary: Tesla is reportedly experiencing significant challenges with its robotaxi service in Austin, with customers facing long wait times and booking difficulties. Bloomberg's test rides revealed waits of up to 30 minutes, indicating a limited operational capacity with fewer than 60 vehicles. This suggests that Tesla's robotaxi deployment is facing scalability issues in its early stages.

title: Is the 2026 Subaru Uncharted a Good Crossover EV? 5 Pros, 2 Cons
summary: The 2026 Subaru Uncharted is a new crossover EV introduced by Subaru, boasting over 300 miles of range and a competitive starting price. It’s part of a joint venture with Toyota, sharing many characteristics with the Toyota C-HR but tuned with Subaru's outdoor persona. This review highlights five pros, including reasonable range, light off-roading capability, good tech, decent value, and cabin comfort, alongside two cons: the rapidly increasing price for AWD and its limited off-road capability compared to traditional Subarus.

title: VinFast posts wider first-quarter loss as revenue and deliveries miss expectations (VFS)
summary: VinFast (VFS) reported a wider first-quarter loss, with revenue and electric vehicle deliveries falling short of expectations. The company recorded a net loss of $641 million, an increase from both the previous quarter and the same period last year. Despite a 26% year-over-year rise in revenue, it missed analyst estimates, as did its EV deliveries which totaled 9,689 units.

title: General Motors plots sodium-ion tech leapfrog into grid-scale energy storage market
summary: General Motors (GM) is entering the grid-scale energy storage market by partnering with sodium-ion battery startup Peak Energy and making a strategic investment in the company. GM will develop prototype sodium-ion cells for grid applications at its Michigan R&D facility, aiming for production this year. This move is part of GM's broader distributed energy strategy, which also includes LFP cell production for near-term demand, bidirectional vehicle-to-grid (V2G) applications, and second-life battery energy storage systems (BESS).

title: Autonomous Trucking Stock Aurora Is Finally Hitting the Fast Lane
summary: Aurora Innovation's stock is gaining momentum, with shares up over 60% this year, as the company expands its partnerships in the autonomous trucking sector. Morningstar analyst Seth Goldstein is optimistic about Aurora's future, predicting a significant ramp-up in autonomous long-haul trucking by the end of 2026 and potential for exponential growth over the next decade. Despite regulatory hurdles and initial stock struggles, Aurora is shifting to a more profitable "driver as a service" model and is positioned as a market leader, with Goldstein setting a fair value estimate of $12 per share.

title: Brand Asset Management Group Inc. Has $350,000 Holdings in Tesla, Inc. $TSLA
summary: Brand Asset Management Group Inc. significantly reduced its stake in Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) by 83.2% in the fourth quarter, now holding 778 shares valued at $350,000. This reduction comes amid other institutional investors adjusting their positions and recent insider selling, including transactions by Tesla's Director Kathleen Wilson-Thompson and CFO Vaibhav Taneja. Despite a 3% drop in share price, analysts maintain a "Hold" rating with an average target price of $404.37, driven by mixed sentiments regarding Tesla's valuation and future prospects in autonomy and energy.

title: Elon Musk Praises Tesla's 'Awesome' AI Chip Team, Says AI6 May Set New Wafer Intelligence Record
summary: Elon Musk lauded Tesla's AI chip design team, stating that their AI6 chip could achieve a new record for usable intelligence from a wafer due to its focus on manufacturability. This strategy aims to maximize computing power from each production run by designing chips that are more resilient to defects. Tesla plans for the AI6 chip to double performance over its predecessor and intends to collaborate with Samsung and TSMC for its production, as part of a larger push towards reducing reliance on external foundries.

title: ETB: Good Income Fund, But High Tech Exposure Could Be A Risk
summary: The Eaton Vance Tax-Managed Buy-Write Income Fund (ETB) offers an 8.33% yield through an index call-writing strategy, providing income and mitigating downside risk. While it has underperformed the S&P 500 in recent bull markets, its strategy may be beneficial in volatile conditions. The fund's sector allocation, with lower tech and higher financials/energy exposure, provides diversification against sector-specific risks, and it currently trades at an 8.22% NAV discount.

title: EHang Holdings Ltd. Sponsored ADR Class A Trade Ideas — TRADEGATE:3UIA
summary: This article compiles various trade ideas and analyses for EHang Holdings Ltd. (EH) from different traders on TradingView. Discussions range from technical analysis and price predictions to reactions to a short report by Wolfpack Research, which alleged fabricated revenues and empty facilities. Traders share long-term bullish outlooks, caution against volatility, and provide strategies for managing risk when trading EH stock.

title: TSLA Stock Hits 1-Month Low Amid SpaceX Merger Jitters — But Analyst Sees Tesla Holders Getting 66% Of Deal Within A Year
summary: Tesla (TSLA) stock hit a one-month low due to speculation about a potential merger with SpaceX. Despite market jitters, Morningstar predicts that Tesla shareholders could receive 66% of a combined entity, noting increasing collaboration between the two companies in AI and other areas. However, analysts also point out significant risks, including shareholder opposition and regulatory challenges from Tesla's China operations.

title: [Form 4] Tesla, Inc. Insider Trading Activity
summary: Tesla's Chief Financial Officer, Vaibhav Taneja, engaged in insider trading activity, selling 2,605.5 shares of common stock valued at approximately $1.05 million. This sale was primarily to satisfy tax withholding obligations related to the vesting of restricted stock units. The Form 4 filing details the transactions and Taneja's remaining direct and indirect holdings in Tesla stock.

title: REX TSLA Growth & Income ETF announces weekly distribution of $0.1970
summary: REX TSLA Growth & Income ETF has announced a weekly distribution of $0.1970 per share for its shareholders. This distribution will be paid on a specified future date to shareholders of record as of a particular cutoff date. The ex-dividend date is also provided.

title: Avoiding Lag: Real-Time Signals in (TSLP) Movement
summary: This article provides a real-time signal analysis for Kurv Yield Premium Strategy Tesla ETF (TSLP), highlighting a weak near and mid-term sentiment that could challenge a positive long-term outlook. It details specific institutional trading strategies, including position, momentum breakout, and risk hedging, along with multi-timeframe signal analysis and support/resistance levels. The analysis aims to help investors optimize position sizing and minimize drawdown risk.

title: A Nissan Leaf by Another Name: Mitsubishi Unveils 2027 Eclipse Sportback EV
summary: Mitsubishi has unveiled images of the 2027 Eclipse Sportback, an electric vehicle based on the redesigned 2026 Nissan Leaf. While technical details and pricing are not yet available, Mitsubishi confirmed cosmetic changes will differentiate it from the Leaf. The Eclipse Sportback is expected to go on sale by early fall 2026, marking Mitsubishi's return to the all-electric vehicle market since the i-MiEV was discontinued after the 2017 model year.

title: Major auto parts maker open to assembling Chinese vehicles in Canada
summary: Magna International, a major global automotive supplier headquartered in Canada, is open to assembling Chinese vehicles in Canada if the plans are long-term. CEO Swamy Kotagiri emphasized that Magna aims to grow with Chinese automakers expanding globally, leveraged by their existing production and systemic knowledge from nearly two decades of operation in China. This strategy follows Magna's recent contract to build two models for Chinese EV maker XPENG in Austria and aligns with growing ties between Canadian companies and Chinese automakers due to shifting trade dynamics.

title: What to know about upcoming SpaceX IPO
summary: SpaceX is filing its S-1 prospectus to go public via an IPO, aiming to raise nearly $75 billion with a target valuation of $1.8 trillion. The article explains the IPO process, where private companies offer shares to public investors, and highlights the risks involved, such as demand uncertainty, price volatility, and historically mixed returns for newly public companies. Frank Newman, Portfolio Director at Ally Invest, advises investors to conduct thorough research and consider if an IPO fits their investment strategy and risk tolerance, also mentioning diversification strategies.

title: Tesla Wins Denmark Green Light For FSD Expansion — But TSLA Stock Slips
summary: Tesla has received approval from the Danish Road Traffic Authority for its Full Self-Driving (FSD) driver assistance software to be used on public roads in Denmark. This expansion follows similar approvals in other European nations like the Netherlands, Lithuania, and Estonia, though EU-wide approval is still pending. Despite this progress in FSD expansion, Tesla's stock (TSLA) slipped 4%, and retail sentiment remained bearish, amid distractions and delays related to other company ventures.

title: Tesla (TSLA) Rolls Out Unsupervised Robotaxis in Austin Metro Area, Reuters Reports
summary: Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) is expanding its unsupervised robotaxi service throughout the Austin Metro area, a move reported by Reuters in early June. This initiative is central to Tesla's growth strategy, which has pivoted towards AI and robotics, aiming to accelerate its autonomous ride-hailing operations. Additionally, Estonia's transport authority recently cleared Tesla's FSD driver-assistance system for use on its roads, though it remains a Level 2 system requiring driver responsibility.

title: Yikes! Elon Musk's Robotaxi Fleet Is Smaller Than Pepsi's Doritos Truck Fleet
summary: Despite Elon Musk's emphasis on autonomy, PepsiCo has a larger fleet of fully driverless trucks operating on public roads than Tesla's unsupervised robotaxi service, according to a recent Wall Street Journal report. PepsiCo's 41 driverless trucks, used for hauling snacks and beverages, have logged zero accidents and high on-time rates, while Tesla's robotaxi fleet is significantly smaller and faces skepticism from prediction markets regarding its future deployment. Tesla's market capitalization relies heavily on its autonomy thesis, but meaningful robotaxi fleet expansion and FSD software release are delayed.

title: Trump Media stock hits 52-week low at 3.8 USD
summary: Trump Media & Technology Group’s equity warrants (DJTWW) have fallen to a 52-week low of $3.82, marking a significant 72% decline from their peak of $13.48. The company's stock has also seen a substantial 69.84% drop over the past year, with a 9.5% decrease in the last week alone, reflecting ongoing market pressures and negative investor sentiment.

title: With The SpaceX IPO Imminent, Nvidia And Tesla Accelerate The Race For Physical AI
summary: Nvidia and Tesla are leading the race in physical AI, pursuing different strategies to dominate the sector as the SpaceX IPO approaches. Nvidia focuses on being an "arms dealer" by providing platform infrastructure, partnering with hardware manufacturers globally, including China's Unitree Robotics. In contrast, Tesla under Elon Musk aims for vertical integration, building the entire AI stack for its Optimus robot, leveraging its existing FSD technology. This competition highlights the distinct approaches of the U.S. and China in developing embodied intelligence, with implications for labor economics, supply chain control, and national security, especially if a Tesla-SpaceX merger occurs.

title: Solidion Strengthens Battery Moat with 7 New Patents
summary: Solidion Technology, Inc. announced it has been granted seven new U.S. patents for anode materials used in lithium batteries, bringing its total U.S. patents to 130. The company's anode technology aims to enhance AI data center uptime with higher energy density, lower costs, and domestically sourced materials. Solidion also raised $35 million through a stock sale to commercialize its Extreme-Climate Battery technology.

title: McDonald’s Stock Could Be Bottoming, Creating a Contrarian Bet for Long-Term Investors
summary: McDonald's stock (MCD) appears to be establishing a technical floor around $275-$280 after a period of consolidation, despite a recent 10% decline. Technical indicators show signs of bottoming, with a bullish divergence in the percentage price oscillator. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating, with an average price target of $333, suggesting a potential 20% upside for this defensive, low-beta stock.

title: Scott Galloway Says Tesla, Nvidia, Broadcom Could Get Caught In The Crossfire Of The Space X IPO
summary: NYU professor Scott Galloway warns that upcoming IPOs from companies like SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI could create a significant "supply flood" in the equity market, potentially leading to an 80% collapse for one of these new market debuts. He suggests that investors reallocating funds to these new offerings might sell off existing holdings, impacting companies like Tesla, Nvidia, and Broadcom. While some analysts believe the market is large enough to absorb this, Galloway draws parallels to past tech booms and busts, indicating a potential correction.

title: 2027 Rivian R2 First Drive: Time to Trade in Your Tesla
summary: The 2027 Rivian R2 is presented as a highly compelling mid-size electric SUV that offers a blend of luxury, capability, and technology at a competitive price, aiming to significantly expand Rivian's market reach. The article details its various trims, pricing, impressive interior space, advanced onboard tech, and excellent on-road and off-road driving performance. The author suggests the R2 is a well-rounded electric SUV without the common compromises found in competitors, positioning it as a strong contender in the EV market.

title: Brand Engagement Network forms AI health joint venture with Intervent International
summary: Brand Engagement Network (NASDAQ:BNAI) has established Intervent Health AI, Inc., a 50/50 joint venture with Intervent International, LLC. This new entity will focus on developing and commercializing AI-powered health coaching solutions, combining Brand Engagement Network’s conversational AI with Intervent’s clinical methodologies. The venture includes exclusive commercialization agreements for North America through SKYE AI USA, LLC, and non-exclusive reseller arrangements internationally, with both founding companies holding equal ownership stakes.

title: What Happens If Tesla and SpaceX Merge?
summary: Morningstar analysts examine the potential for a merger between Tesla and SpaceX, driven by Elon Musk's desire for consolidation and increasing AI collaboration between the companies. While a merger presents strong business cases like streamlined operations and shared AI development, significant hurdles exist, including divergent long-term goals, regulatory concerns, and especially the challenge of agreeing on valuation and equity distribution that satisfies both Tesla and SpaceX shareholders, given SpaceX's high valuation multiples. They expect discussions to intensify after SpaceX's IPO.

title: Wolfspeed’s new chip tech designed to boost EV range and charging efficiency
summary: Wolfspeed has introduced its fifth generation of Silicon Carbide (SiC) MOSFET technology, which significantly improves efficiency for 1200 V and 750 V automotive and industrial applications. This new Gen 5 technology, built on a 200 mm manufacturing platform, reduces specific on-resistance by up to 27%, leading to increased EV mileage range, better charging infrastructure efficiency, and more compact system designs. The technology also enhances durability with an improved junction temperature and provides a low-risk path to market for customers.

title: Tesla, Inc. $TSLA Shares Acquired by Focus Partners Advisor Solutions LLC
summary: Focus Partners Advisor Solutions LLC significantly increased its stake in Tesla ($TSLA) by 10.1% in the fourth quarter, bringing its total holdings to $23.22 million. Several other institutional investors also adjusted their positions in the electric vehicle producer. The article also highlights recent positive and neutral news surrounding Tesla, including analyst ratings, price targets, and insider trading activities.
